modules.title: Forces
modules.html:
A force is a push or pull that changes (or tries to change) the state of motion of a body. Unit: newton (N).

Types

  • Contact forces: friction, tension, normal, applied.
  • Non-contact (field) forces: gravity, magnetic, electrostatic.

Friction

Force opposing relative motion. F = μR where μ is coefficient of friction and R the normal reaction.

Static friction > kinetic friction. Friction is useful (walking, brakes) and harmful (wear, heat).

examples.q: Block of 10 kg on floor, μ=0.2. Friction (g=10)?

modules.title: Newton's laws of motion
modules.html:
1st law (inertia): a body continues at rest or in uniform motion unless acted on by a net external force.

2nd law: F = ma (net force = mass × acceleration). Equivalently F = Δp/Δt.

3rd law: to every action there is an equal and opposite reaction.

examples.a: 3rd law — gas pushes bullet forward, bullet pushes gun back.

modules.title: Momentum and impulse
modules.html:
Momentum p = mv, unit kg·m/s.
Impulse J = FΔt = change in momentum = mΔv.

Conservation of linear momentum: in the absence of external forces, total momentum before = total momentum after collision.

Elastic collision: momentum AND kinetic energy conserved. Inelastic: only momentum conserved.

examples.q: 2 kg mass at 3 m/s hits and sticks to 1 kg at rest. Final velocity?

modules.title: Work, energy, power
modules.html:
Work W = F·s·cosθ (joule). Kinetic energy KE = ½mv². Potential energy PE = mgh.

Conservation of energy: energy is neither created nor destroyed; it changes from one form to another.

Power P = W/t = F·v (watt).

examples.q: Lift 20 kg through 3 m (g=10). Work done?
Engine does 6000 J of work in 10 s. Power?

modules.title: Simple machines
modules.html:
A machine makes work easier. Key ratios:

  • Mechanical Advantage MA = Load / Effort
  • Velocity Ratio VR = distance moved by effort / distance moved by load
  • Efficiency η = (MA / VR) × 100%

Examples

  • Single fixed pulley: VR = 1.
  • Single movable pulley: VR = 2.
  • Block-and-tackle: VR = number of ropes supporting the load.
  • Inclined plane: VR = length / height.
  • Screw/jack: VR = 2πR / pitch.

examples.q: Load 40 N, effort 25 N, VR=2. MA and efficiency?
VR of a block-and-tackle with 4 supporting ropes?
